item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love how this is essentially a completely natural phenomenon that's anomalous only because the Foundation noticed it somehow. (Well, the fast decomposition is definitely anomalous, that's not what I'm getting at here.) If humans hadn't gotten caught up in this by mistake, it would have just kept right on going and everything would be fine.

That said… It's weird, because once we get to the dead deer sprouting deer food, I totally get it. And yet, this feels like it's too short? I was kind of expecting a much longer article. Maybe it's the brevity of Rothenberger's note, but even that's fine because you prime us with information about what happened to him at the start of the article.

Well, it definitely works, so +1 from me. I also fixed a few things for ya.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find this SCP brief, but pretty interesting. I think further elaborating on the effects of 5523 on people would be interesting, but at the same time what is left to the imagination can often be better. Regardless, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After some changes, I now like the piece much better. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like this is missing some story development, and has some writing issues.

On the story development side, this is basically a thing that compels deer to eat it, then die. The twist is that this is a good thing, apparently. I don't really get why that should be. As described, the deer are going extinct very rapidly, because they die less than 24 hours after birth, as do the mothers. More exposition is needed to show why this rapid extinction of the deer is good, ecologically speaking. Or to show why the deer don't go extinct.

On the writing side:
Fawn should be fawns, I think.
Footnote 5 needs a -1
Saying "Rothenberg" all over the place seems a bit non-clinical.
SCP-5523 seems to be much more than just a behavioral change in female deer - other anomalous behavior includes the seeds in the breast milk, the rapid decomposition, the rapid growth, and the hallucinations experienced by Sid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be honest, I still don't get it. However, judging from the voting situation, I felt that my ignorance was the reason why I did not find this article interesting, so I canceled DV.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for letting me know your thoughts. First off, my apologies for adding the 5000 tag, I thought that just went to any article in series six. My apologies, it has been removed.

To be clear, the general idea is that the majority of forests in Alberta, and pretty much everywhere where deer are, created by deer themselves. Deers are herbavores, and as such eat plants native to forests and the sort. In a way, they're repaying the earth, for food, by creating more plants following death.

I added a couple of sections to better explain -1 instances. Anything else you need clarifying? Anything that would help you like the piece more?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't quite understand what was interesting about this article. Is there any hidden meaning? Also, what SCP-5523-1 refers to is not defined.

I also don't know why the tag contains "5000".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you to both Rokurokubi and TrustyOlValet2 for feedback, as well as a special thanks to Valet for fixing an error.

Image is not mine, it is taken from here, and is CC okay.

https://commons.m.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cow_Skeleton_-_geograph.org.uk_-_26234.jpg
